hook: ChatGPT gave me a 5-step plan. I needed a 5-minute rant.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 5-step plan. I needed a 5-minute rant.

You know the moment. You're overwhelmed, you dump everything into the chat — the situation, the backstory, the frustration — and it comes back with:

"Here are 4 actionable steps to move forward."

Step 1: Identify the core issue.

BRO. I KNOW WHAT THE ISSUE IS. That's why I'm here.

Some people process by getting structured back. Totally valid.

But some people process by being *heard* first — and THEN getting the framework.

AI defaults to solution mode because that's what it's trained to look useful doing. It doesn't know you need validation before strategy. It doesn't know you think out loud. It doesn't know that a bulleted list right now feels like being handed a pamphlet at a funeral.

This is the gap BootFile solves.

When your AI profile knows you're a verbal processor who needs to feel understood before you can receive advice — it stops skipping to the answer.

Same AI. Completely different conversation.

If you've ever closed a chat feeling MORE frustrated than when you opened it, your AI doesn't know how you think yet.

It should.
